text: Assistant: Both repos are clear. Trellis is a **deterministic reconciliation engine** — state in, effect plans + receipts out, with an explicit design goal of being "legible to tooling and AI agents that need to reason about why a system did what it did, without parsing logs." tenex-edge is a Rust/Nostr daemon that **derives live resources** (relay subscriptions, 30315 status events, presence, hook context) from changing state. That's a promising surface. Let me dig into the real tenex-edge code with parallel Sonnet agents. [...] **Not bumped** — stayed at `1`. Both tables are pure additive `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` with no alteration to any existing table, and `schema.rs`'s own header comment establishes that additive tables don't require a version bump (the version gate exists to catch breaking/destructive changes, not additions). Flagging this per instructions in case a concurrent main-line change also wants the version bumped — reconcile if so.
